ours.label: Neuron Companion
ours.notes: In-CRM conversational companion (stateless Python/FastAPI, secondary interface). Umbrella capability for the F0038 epic first slice. Engine remains source of truth; Neuron calls it as the user (forwarded token).

rationale.summary: A2A-aligned internal profile (not ad hoc Python delegation, not a public federation) avoids a migration cliff when heads/agents later move across process boundaries, while keeping F0038 shippable.

theirs.label: Neuron Companion

ours.label: Neuron Companion Telemetry
ours.notes: Establishes the baseline needs-attention→draft-ready duration plus a minimal secondary v1 set (DAU, %-actioned, drafts/mock-sent). Fire-and-forget to Serilog; PII-minimized (correlation ids + metric fields only).

theirs.label: Neuron Companion Telemetry

ours.label: Day-at-a-Glance Multi-Zone Shell
ours.notes: Multi-zone frame; assembly, not composition (no cross-zone ranking — that is the deferred Day-at-a-Glance brain). Renewals live, three inert stubs.

ours.label: Versioned Multi-Part Message/Component Envelope
ours.notes: Versioned envelope (text|app|status|sources|actions) keyed by thread_id so persisted threads replay correctly. Component-registry rendering only.

rationale.summary: A2A messages/parts/artifacts map onto the envelope part types; the frontend renders only registered components from validated props — Neuron never returns executable markup or model-emitted numbers.

theirs.label: Versioned Multi-Part Message/Component Envelope
theirs.notes: Versioned envelope (text|app|status|sources|actions) keyed by thread_id so persisted threads replay correctly. Component-registry rendering only.

rationale.summary: A2A messages/parts/artifacts map onto the envelope part types; the frontend renders only registered components from validated props — Neuron never returns executable markup or model-emitted numbers.

conflicts.message: added on both sides with different content

ours.label: Neuron-Owned Operation Persistence (neuron.* schema)
ours.notes: Neuron owns and writes its own neuron.* schema directly (threads, messages, message_parts, agent_runs, tool_calls, provenance_events) — not via an engine pass-through. Service stays stateless; threads owner-scoped.

rationale.summary: Six neuron.* tables; definitions (Agent Cards/prompts) stay checked-in assets referenced by id+version+hash; cross-store writes are engine-first authoritative with an idempotent Neuron record referencing the engine id (no cross-runtime transaction; outbox deferred).

theirs.label: Neuron-Owned Operation Persistence (neuron.* schema)
theirs.notes: Neuron owns and writes its own neuron.* schema directly (threads, messages, message_parts, agent_runs, tool_calls, provenance_events) — not via an engine pass-through. Service stays stateless; threads owner-scoped.

rationale.summary: Six neuron.* tables; definitions (Agent Cards/prompts) stay checked-in assets referenced by id+version+hash; cross-store writes are engine-first authoritative with an idempotent Neuron record referencing the engine id (no cross-runtime transaction; outbox deferred).

ours.label: Neuron Orchestration Runtime (bootstrap, registries, YAML plans, A2A profile)
ours.notes: First runnable stateless runtime: specialist-head registry, tool registry, versioned YAML orchestration assets (schema-validated, fail-fast on unknown head/tool or missing terminal state), private Agent Card registry, A2A task trace. Heads live under neuron/crm_agents/.

rationale.summary: Simple versioned YAML + registered Agent Cards is enough for F0038; MS Agent Framework is an unnecessary runtime dependency. Every referenced head/tool/terminal state must resolve or the asset is refused.

theirs.label: Neuron Orchestration Runtime (bootstrap, registries, YAML plans, A2A profile)
theirs.notes: First runnable stateless runtime: specialist-head registry, tool registry, versioned YAML orchestration assets (schema-validated, fail-fast on unknown head/tool or missing terminal state), private Agent Card registry, A2A task trace. Heads live under neuron/crm_agents/.

rationale.summary: Simple versioned YAML + registered Agent Cards is enough for F0038; MS Agent Framework is an unnecessary runtime dependency. Every referenced head/tool/terminal state must resolve or the asset is refused.

ours.label: Renewal Outreach Drafter (goal agent + persist + mock-send)
ours.notes: The one write. Proactive CTA → generate-on-click → persist InternalOnly AI-generated ActivityTimelineEvent → edit in-chat → mock-send (commits Identified→Outreach, fakes SMTP). Underwriter only.

rationale.summary: Dedicated least-privilege renewal:draft_outreach gates draft+mock-send; the engine state machine permits Identified→Outreach for an Underwriter only on the mock-send path, leaving the F0007 Distribution-owned transition model intact.

ours.label: Live Renewals Specialist Head
ours.notes: The one live zone in F0038. Reads renewals needing attention (Identified/ Outreach within 90d of expiry, urgency-ordered, no-contact-30d flag) and per-renewal drill context, permission-respecting via the engine.

theirs.label: Live Renewals Specialist Head

ours.label: CRM Scope Guard (out_of_scope redirect)
ours.notes: CRM-scoped assistant (intake L8), not a general chatbot. Non-CRM intents route to a polite CRM redirect; resistant to prompt-injection; fails safe to the bounded CRM path. Guard decisions recorded in the operation store.

theirs.label: CRM Scope Guard (out_of_scope redirect)

ours.label: Zone-Dispatch (multi-head extension point)
ours.notes: Orchestrator enumerates registered specialist heads and dispatches per zone; live heads return content, stubs return a typed inactive payload. This is the F0040 extension point ("flip a stub to live"). Head contract is thin and provisional — do not gold-plate (a stub agrees with anything).

rationale.summary: Mock-send commits the real Identified→Outreach WorkflowTransition + a 'sent (simulated)' ActivityTimelineEvent atomically, but fakes SMTP — no real email. Reuses event:renewal-transitioned for the transition itself.
